steps.title: Sketch the outer window rectangle (pencil)
steps.description: Lightly sketch a large rectangle for the shop window. In one-point perspective the far vertical edge is ever-so-slightly shorter than the near one — keep the difference subtle. If the window is viewed head-on, both verticals are simply equal.

steps.title: Add the window frame and muntin (pencil)
steps.description: Sketch an equal-width frame around all four sides. Then add a single horizontal muntin — a crossbar — roughly two-thirds of the way up, dividing the window into a taller upper pane and a shorter lower pane.

steps.title: Sketch the display platform (pencil)
steps.description: Draw a low shallow rectangle at the bottom of the lower pane — the raised platform where merchandise is displayed. It sits inside the window, set back from the glass.

steps.title: Switch to ink and trace the window frame
steps.description: Switch to ink. Ink the outer frame cleanly, then the muntin. These lines should feel precise — a window frame is a manufactured object, and its edges are straight.

steps.title: Ink the platform and suggest the contents (ink)
steps.description: Ink the display platform edge. Then add two or three simple shapes on it — a tall rectangle for a standing display, a small cylinder, a flat square card. Suggest merchandise without committing to what it is. The viewer fills in the rest.
